steps.title: Stay within Hemingway
steps.content: Blatt's conclusion is about a negative correlation within Hemingway's own body of work. The strongest support should compare Hemingway novels with different reputations and different rates of -ly adverbs.

steps.title: Reject outside comparisons
steps.content: Choice A brings in Fitzgerald, Choice B brings in Morrison, and Choice C compares one Hemingway novel with a broad statistic about other authors. These do not clearly test the pattern within Hemingway's oeuvre.

steps.title: Match merit and adverb rate
steps.content: Choice D compares an acclaimed Hemingway novel with 63 -ly adverbs per 10,000 words to a less-acclaimed Hemingway novel with 102, supporting the negative correlation. The answer is D.
